steps.title: Identify condition for infinitely many solutions
steps.content:
• The system has infinitely many solutions, so the two equations are scalar multiples.
  • Thus there exists a nonzero constant k such that
$$A = k\cdot117,\quad B = k\cdot78,\quad C = k\cdot65$$

steps.title: Compute (A+C)/B and simplify
steps.content:
• Form the expression (A+C)/B and substitute:
$$\frac{A+C}{B} = \frac{k\cdot117 + k\cdot65}{k\cdot78} = \frac{k(117+65)}{k\cdot78} = \frac{182}{78}$$
  • Reduce the fraction:
$$\frac{182}{78} = \frac{91}{39} = \frac{7}{3}$$The value does not depend on k, so (A+C)/B = 7/3.
The answer is 91/39.

Equivalent forms accepted: 91/39, 2.333, 7/3.
